terms.definition: Things that can grow, breathe, eat, move, feel, and reproduce. Plants, animals, and humans are all living things.
terms.exampleUsage: A mango tree, a parrot, and a fish are all living things because they grow and breathe.

terms.definition: The natural home or environment where a plant or animal lives and finds food, water, and shelter.
terms.exampleUsage: The Gir forest in Gujarat is the habitat of the Asiatic lion.

terms.definition: The process by which green plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to make their own food and release oxygen.
terms.exampleUsage: Leaves look green because of a substance that helps in photosynthesis.

terms.definition: A special body feature or behaviour that helps a living thing survive in its habitat.
terms.exampleUsage: A camel's wide feet are an adaptation that helps it walk on desert sand without sinking.

terms.definition: An animal that eats only plants and plant products for food.
terms.exampleUsage: A cow is a herbivore because it eats grass and other plants.

terms.definition: An animal that eats only other animals for food.
terms.exampleUsage: A tiger is a carnivore because it hunts and eats deer and other animals.

terms.definition: The process by which living things produce young ones or new living things of the same kind.
terms.exampleUsage: A hen reproduces by laying eggs that hatch into chicks.

terms.definition: Tiny holes on the surface of leaves through which plants breathe, taking in carbon dioxide and releasing oxygen.
terms.exampleUsage: Stomata open and close to control how much air and water the plant exchanges.

terms.definition: Special organs in fish and some water animals that help them breathe by taking oxygen from water.
terms.exampleUsage: Fish use their gills to breathe underwater, just like we use our lungs to breathe air.

terms.definition: A safe place where living things can hide from danger, rest, and protect their young ones.
terms.exampleUsage: A bird's nest is a shelter where it keeps its eggs and baby birds safe.

terms.definition: An animal that eats both plants and other animals for food.
terms.exampleUsage: A bear is an omnivore because it eats fish as well as berries and honey.

terms.definition: The ability of a living thing to blend in with its surroundings so that it is hard to see, helping it hide from predators or catch prey.
terms.exampleUsage: A chameleon uses camouflage by changing its colour to match the leaves and branches around it.
